ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag. Card is a Wizards-original Duskmourn horror creature (generic Lizard common). No verification needed.

trivia_bullets.text: Duskmourn: House of Horror is the 102nd MTG expansion (released 2024-09-27) and the second set in the Dragonstorm Arc. The plane is a single haunted house grown to swallow an entire world, ruled by the demon Valgavoth; Wizards pitched the set as a 1970s–1990s slasher / horror-film homage. [MTG Wiki: Duskmourn: House of Horror]
sources: MTG Wiki: Duskmourn: House of Horror

trivia_bullets.text: The plane originally had two suns (one greater, one lesser) which Valgavoth consumed as the House grew a tower that pierced the clouds; with the suns gone, crops failed and beasts starved, accelerating the plane's collapse. The low amber sun looming behind the Branchsnapper is consistent with this swallowed-sun motif rather than ordinary daylight. [MTG Wiki: Duskmourn (plane), via WebSearch snippet citing Wizards lore summary]

Illustrated by John Tedrick, whose other Duskmourn contributions include Hauntwoods Shrieker, Twitching Doll, and Scrabbling Skullcrab. The piece is publicly archived on his ArtStation as part of his Duskmourn portfolio. [ArtStation: tedrick94 / Duskmourn — Slavering Branchsnapper]

A vanilla-with-cycling green common: 7/6 Trample for {4}{G}{G}, plus Forestcycling {2} (search a Forest). EDHREC lists the card in 7,006 Commander decks at retrieval time with current edhrec_rank 12,391, which is bulk-common territory — its play pattern is overwhelmingly cycle-it-for-a-Forest in green ramp shells rather than hard-cast it. Standard/Pioneer/Modern/Legacy/Pauper/Commander legal. [EDHREC: slavering-branchsnapper], [Scryfall]

Flavor text: "Hey, look! It made us a nice big path through the hedge maze!" — Zimone. Zimone Wola is a Quandrix / Strixhaven prodigy who solved the Vorzani Conjecture (a magical fractal theorem), giving her a non-Planeswalker grasp of multiverse structure; she is among the named survivors trapped inside Duskmourn during the Dragonstorm Arc, and the small fleeing figures in the hedge-maze corridor read as her survivor band putting the beast's rampage to use rather than fleeing it. [Scryfall flavor_text], [MTG Wiki: Zimone Wola]

The grid-cross emblem the vision pass observed superimposed on the amber sun is NOT documented in any source we could reach as a named recurring Duskmourn iconography (no Wizards article, MTG Wiki page, or Draftsim art-roundup names a sun-with-grid-cross symbol). It may simply be a window-frame silhouette or a stylized eclipse rendered by Tedrick rather than a set-wide motif. Flag for the curator: investigate downstream if more Duskmourn cards land in inventory showing the same emblem. [Negative result from MTG Wiki / Wizards articles / WebSearch]
sources: MTG Wiki: Duskmourn: House of Horror (no mention)

related_cards.name: Zimone, All-Questioning
related_cards.note: Zimone's named legendary creature card in DSK; she is the speaker of this card's flavor text and ties the Branchsnapper scene to the Strixhaven-survivors-in-Duskmourn narrative thread.

related_cards.name: Hauntwoods Shrieker
related_cards.note: Another John Tedrick Duskmourn piece; useful for an artist-cycle bundle.

related_cards.name: Valgavoth, Harrower of Souls
related_cards.note: The set's antagonist; the consumed-suns lore originates from his Ascension. Anchor card for any Duskmourn-lore bundle.
